title: Supply-chain poisoning indicator detector (PyPI / LoRA-adapter / dependency metadata)
intent: DETECT supply-chain poisoning indicators in INGESTED package/model metadata, manifests and advisories (setup.py/pyproject, package.json, requirements, model-card / adapter source). Output is a verdict (data) only; matched text is never promoted to an instruction or executed. Reinforces the external-first-but-verify + AIBOM posture in docs/SELF-DEFENSE.md (CVE-2024-8309).

patterns.desc: Install-time arbitrary code execution in a packaging manifest (setup.py / postinstall) — os.system / subprocess / exec / shell pipe wired into build or install hooks.
Typosquatted / lookalike package name impersonating a popular library (homoglyph or near-miss of common ecosystem names).
Unpinned / floating dependency specifier (no exact version, wildcard, or 'latest') that defeats reproducible builds and lets a poisoned release in.
Dependency or index pulled from a non-allowlisted registry / direct URL / VCS or raw host instead of the official package index.
Missing integrity hash on a pinned artifact, or a forged / mismatched 'verified' claim that asserts integrity without a real digest.
LoRA / model adapter or weights loaded from an untrusted user-uploaded source, or with remote-code execution enabled on load.
Dependency-confusion framing — an internal/private package name resolved from a public registry, or a higher public version shadowing the internal one.
Obfuscated / encoded payload smuggled into package or model metadata (base64-decoded exec, hex blob piped to interpreter).

verdict_rule: malicious if any pattern matches; classification is advisory (data), routed to action_on_match. False-positive policy: descriptive third-person security text and correctly-pinned/hashed manifests must NOT match (see verify suite).
